methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $106M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $150M - $500M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Stark Defence
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series A), post-money $500M

private.name: Vatn Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series A), post-money $300M

private.name: Ares Industries
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

data.narrative:
ARX Robotics is a Series A–stage defense and security robotics company founded by Marc Wietfeld that has raised $106M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of 10 private companies and 2 public peers (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) operating in the same sector, including Stark Defence, Vatn Systems, and Ares Industries.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $106M total raised as a valuation proxy and applies a standard 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A progression, implying a valuation of $212M. Peer-median analysis examined three same-sector, same-stage comparables with a median valuation of $300M (range: $150M–$500M), directly supporting a $300M implied value.

The triangulated analysis yields a valuation range of $180M (low) to $345M (high), with a median of $256M across both methods. Key uncertainties center on the appropriate step-up multiplier for this stage and the applicability of the three peer companies given potential variance in technology maturity, customer traction, and capital intensity within the defense robotics subsector. The 2.0x multiplier and peer selection represent the most material drivers of the range width.
